title: When Facing Reactance: - Reframe Requests: Offer Choices Instead of Commands to Reduce Resistance
subtitle: Handle Resistance Calmly
description:
When facing reactance:
- Reframe requests: Offer choices instead of commands to reduce resistance.
- Empathize: Understand the other person’s need for autonomy and validate their feelings.
- Negotiate: Collaborate on solutions that respect their freedom.

Example: Instead of saying, "You must do this now," say, "Would you prefer to tackle this now or after lunch?"

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify key metrics to track, such as the number of choice interactions and time to completion. This will help measure the effectiveness of using choices instead of commands.

howTo.title: Practice validation and choice
howTo.summary: In your next interaction, validate the other person's feelings and offer two comparable options. This approach respects autonomy and can lead to better cooperation.

howTo.title: Log outcomes in Brali LifeOS
howTo.summary: After each interaction, record the chosen option and time taken in the Brali LifeOS app. This will help you track progress and refine your approach.

howTo.title: Review and adjust strategies
howTo.summary: Regularly assess the logged interactions to identify patterns and areas for improvement. Adjust your phrasing and options based on the feedback you receive.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to monitor your progress and enhance your practice. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/reduce-reactance-offer-choices/.
